questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 4 cm × 5 cm × 8 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height = 4 × 5 × 8 = 160 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 3 inches and a height of 10 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = π(3)²(10) = 90π cubic inches.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 6 meters and a height of 9 meters. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(6)²(9) = (1/3)π(36)(9) = 108π cubic meters.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 5 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π(5)³ = (4/3)π(125) = 500π/3 cubic centimeters.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 24 square inches and a height (length) of 7 inches. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height = 24 × 7 = 168 cubic inches.

questions.question: A cylindrical tank is filled with water to a height of 12 feet. The tank has a radius of 4 feet. If water is drained until the height is 8 feet, how many cubic feet of water were removed? Use π ≈ 3.14.
questions.explanation: Volume removed = π × r² × Δh = 3.14 × (4)² × (12 − 8) = 3.14 × 16 × 4 ≈ 50.24 cubic feet.
